elements.text: Standards for Notarization
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Certain loan documents and instruments may require notarization under applicable laws to allow for either recognition, enforcement, or recordation of the loan document or instrument.  Except as otherwise permitted in this Guide, and subject to all applicable legal requirements imposed by the state where the real property is located, all loan documents and instruments must be notarized in accordance with and legally valid under the laws and regulations of the state in which the notarization is performed at the time it was performed in order to be eligible for sale to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In certain situations, federal laws and regulations permit duly authorized officials of the U.S. Department of State and the U.S. Department of Defense to effect notarial acts in the performance of their duties. Subject to any applicable legal requirements imposed by the state where the real property is located, such notarization by these authorized officials is eligible on the same basis as that performed in accordance with state law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: International Notarization and Apostille Process
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Subject to any applicable legal requirements imposed by the state where the real property is located, any international notarization not performed by a duly authorized official of the U.S. is eligible provided it
elements.label: text

elements.text: is in accordance with and legally valid under the laws and regulations of the foreign jurisdiction in which the notarization is performed at the time it was performed, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: contains an apostille certification from a competent authority, as recognized by the U.S. Department of State.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Law of Location of the Mortgaged Property
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: If the law of the state where the real property is located requires notarization, such notarization must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with such state's laws and regulations, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: qualify for recognition under such state's laws.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Requirements in the Selling Guide or Servicing Guide
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: In order for a loan to be eligible for sale to Fannie Mae and where notarization is not explicitly required by applicable law, but only by a requirement in the Selling Guide or Servicing Guide , all loan documents and instruments must have notarization effected by any such notarial method as permitted or recognized as valid by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the law of the state where the real property is located, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: federal law or the law of the state where the notary is registered and acting under applicable notarial authority and requirements of such jurisdiction.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Note: Nothing in this topic is meant to supersede the existing policies in this Guide with respect to electronic notarization and remote online notarization.  See  A2-4.1-03, Electronic Records, Signatures, and Transactions for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Audio-visual Aided Remote Ink-Signed Notarization
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: A remote ink-signed notarization (RIN) is an audio-visual aided ink-signed method of notarization of paper documents, where the borrower authentication and signature witnessing are accomplished by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the notary and principal's use of real-time audio-visual conferencing technology,
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the signatures are ink-signed, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the notarial seal is applied manually to the applicable documents.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Lenders may allow for the use of real-time audio-video technology to satisfy the physical presence requirements, or where there are no physical presence requirements, under applicable law for notarial acts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements apply to remote ink-signed notarization:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The notarization has been expressly authorized under applicable law.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The notarization is performed in accordance with and is legally valid under the laws and regulations of the state in which the notarization is performed, at the time it was performed.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The borrower or person whose signature is being notarized and the notary are physically located in the state where the notarial act is performed.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The audio-visual method used for the remote ink-signed notarization must meet the minimum standards described below.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: All loans delivered to Fannie Mae that have been closed using audio-visual aided ink-signed notarization must be delivered with special feature code (SFC) 920 in addition to any other special feature codes that may apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Minimum Standards for Audio-visual Methods
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The audio-visual method used for RIN must include the following minimum standards.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Criteria
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum Standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: authentication
elements.label: text

elements.text: It must include a review of
elements.label: text

elements.text: • two forms of government-issued photo ID that have a signature via two-way
elements.label: text

elements.text: audio-video technology and capture of such photo ID[s] via a photocopy or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other electronic image; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: • two-factor identity authentication, using a combination of at least two
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the following factors:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ⚬ remote presentation of a government-issued photo ID that has a
elements.label: text

elements.text: signature,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ⚬ credential analysis, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ⚬ identity-proofing (that is, knowledge-based authentication).
elements.label: text

elements.text: System measures
elements.label: text

elements.text: The system must
elements.label: text

elements.text: • prevent interference with authenticity, integrity, and security of the portions
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the notarial ceremony that are conducted via the audio-video technology,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: • protect the electronic record and backup record from unauthorized use.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Recording capability
elements.label: text

elements.text: It must have the ability to record the portion of the notarial ceremony
elements.label: text

elements.text: conducted via the audio-video technology with storage for the minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: period required by applicable law, or if no period is specified in the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: law, for 10 years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional Requirements:
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The lender must maintain, or cause to be retained, the recording of the portions of the notarial ceremony conducted via the audio-video technology for the greater of 10 years or the minimum period required by applicable law, in accordance with the requirements above.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The mortgage loan may not be a Texas Section 50(a)(6) loan.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If the loan document is required to be recorded, then the county recorder in the state and county where the property is located must accept the document for recording.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: If the document is a security instrument or an amendment to a security instrument, the audio-visual aided remote ink-signed notarization must comply with the title requirements in B7-2-04, Special Title Insurance Coverage Considerations  and the title insurance company may not take any exception for the audio-visual aided remote ink-signed notarization.

elements.text: License to Use Fannie Mae Marks
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: General Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae owns and uses the Fannie Mae trademark, the Fannie Mae logo, the Federal National Mortgage Association trade name, and numerous other trademarks that identify Fannie Mae as the source or sponsor of various products or services, collectively the 'Marks' or the 'Fannie Mae Marks.' elements.text: Subpart A3, Getting Started With Fannie Mae
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: This subpart describes the requirements a lender must meet in order to transact business with Fannie Mae, which includes the procedures for obtaining technology applications and completing the compliance certifications. It contains policies on concurrent servicing transfers and working with third parties, such as mortgage brokers, loan correspondents, quality control firms, document custodians, and subservicers. It addresses Fannie Mae's requirements related to data delivery and integrity, handling of confidential information, fraud prevention, and fidelity bond and errors and omissions coverage.

elements.text: System Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae's technology solutions require a standard hardware and software configuration. To ensure that Fannie Mae's technology tools perform at or above the expected levels as determined by Fannie Mae's performance baseline testing, the lender may need to upgrade its current computer configuration. Review the equipment configurations presented in Technology Requirements to ensure that you meet or exceed them.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : This information does not apply to integration solutions. See Technology Integration for information about lender integration solutions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Registering for an Application
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: To become an approved user of any of Fannie Mae's single-family technology applications, lenders should visit the Technology Manager page for instructions in how to register for an application. Sellers/servicers must execute a Software Subscription Agreement to obtain access to any of Fannie Mae's technology applications. See Software Subscription Agreement in the Consolidated Technology Guide , for more information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Operational Setup
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Fannie Mae provides assistance to all newly approved lenders to ensure that they are set up properly to conduct business with Fannie Mae. Once a lender is approved as a seller or servicer, a senior Fannie Mae marketing consultant contacts the lender to provide information about establishing bank accounts, getting started with Fannie Mae technology, and taking advantage of relevant training.

elements.text: Reporting Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The seller/servicer must notify Fannie Mae if, after conducting due diligence, it determines that a breach of a selling warranty related to compliance with laws has likely occurred. The seller/servicer's notification responsibilities depend on how many loans are affected and whether the breach could warrant a repurchase demand based on the criteria described above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting Category 1
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The seller/servicer must notify Fannie Mae if both of the following conditions occur:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the number of loans affected by the same potential breach exceeds the lesser of 500 loans or 1% of the total number of loans delivered to Fannie Mae in the prior year, and
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: all potentially affected loans were delivered to Fannie Mae within the same calendar quarter.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The notification is required within 60 days of the later of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the end of the calendar quarter in which such loans were delivered, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: discovery of the potential breach.
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: The seller/servicer must report these loans using the self-report functionality in  Loan Quality Connect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note : If the calculation described above results in fewer than five potentially affected loans, then the seller/servicer does not need to notify Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting Category 2
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The following reporting requirements apply when:
elements.label: text

elements.text: the potential breach could warrant a repurchase demand, or
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: the number of loans affected by the same type of potential breach delivered in the same calendar quarter does not exceed the lesser of 500 loans or 1% of the total number of loans delivered to Fannie Mae in the prior year.

elements.text: Examples:
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Scenario 1: A lender identifies a repeated potential breach related to compliance with laws where a repurchase demand is not warranted.
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The lender determines June 1 it may have failed to provide a property valuation when required under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act. This impacted 600 loans that were delivered to Fannie Mae between January 1 and March 31. The lender must report the potential breach to Fannie Mae within 60 days of June 1. In this scenario, the number of loans impacted exceeded 500 loans and the loans were delivered within the same quarter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Scenario 2: A lender that delivered 70,000 loans to Fannie Mae the prior year identifies a repeated potential breach related to compliance with laws where a repurchase demand is not warranted.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: The lender determines August 15 it may have failed to provide a property valuation when required under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act. This impacted 200 loans that were delivered to Fannie Mae between January 1 and March 31, and 400 loans that were delivered to Fannie Mae between April 1 and June 30. The lender will not be required to report the potential breach for either quarter, as the number of loans impacted in each quarter did not exceed 500 loans. In this scenario, 500 loans is less than 700 loans (1% of prior year deliveries).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Scenario 3: A lender identifies a single loan impacted by a potential breach for which a repurchase demand is a potential remedy.
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The lender may have violated the Fair Housing Act. The lender must report the breach to Fannie Mae within 60 days of determination of the potential breach, unless it determines the non-compliance has been remedied or will be remedied within 60 days in accordance with applicable law.

elements.label: text

elements.text: Contacts) .
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a seller/servicer is not subject to the BSA's AML provisions, it
elements.label: text

elements.text: must establish a suspicious activity identification program that
elements.label: text

elements.text: consists of internal policies, procedures, and controls to identify
elements.label: text

elements.text: and report to Fannie Mae suspicious activity, including mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: fraud, money laundering, terrorist financing, and related crimes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A suspicious activity identification program must have the
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: • written policies, procedures, and internal controls effectuating
elements.label: text

elements.text: the program;
elements.label: text

elements.text: • program training for relevant personnel; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: • testing to assess the program's effectiveness.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures, controls and any other material that documents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: implements these elements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All sellers and servicers must report all instances of suspicious
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity related to Fannie Mae loans using the self-report
elements.label: text

elements.text: functionality in Loan Quality Connect or those related to Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae's business activities to Fannie Mae's Mortgage Fraud
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting division (see E-1-02, List of Contacts) .
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: seller/servicers of any owner, including any direct, indirect, or

elements.text: A3-2-02, Responsible Lending Practices (09/01/2021)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Introduction
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This topic contains information on responsible lending practices, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Responsible Lending Policies
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Underwriting Standards
elements.label: list_item

elements.text: Overview
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Each seller/servicer must use prudent, sound, and responsible business practices in its marketing and origination efforts. The seller/servicer's operating policies and procedures must provide an effective means of ensuring responsible lending practices, and identifying and avoiding predatory lending practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers/servicers must update their business practices as necessary to ensure responsible lending practices that are in line with current market conditions. They must have policies and procedures, including quality control procedures, to ensure that loans delivered to Fannie Mae comply with these responsible lending requirements. For quality control requirements, see Part D, Ensuring Quality Control (QC).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Responsible Lending Policies
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Published June 3, 2026
elements.label: page_footer

elements.text: Sellers must comply with the responsible lending practices described in the following table.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Steering
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: • seek to offer the lowest-cost product with the lowest-risk loan terms for which
elements.label: text

elements.text: they qualify,
elements.label: text

elements.text: • not steer borrowers toward a particular loan program to qualify the borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a loan in an effort to misrepresent the borrower's true credit or income
elements.label: text

elements.text: related qualifications, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: • ensure their loan originator compensation practices comply with the loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: originator compensation provisions of the TILA and Regulation Z, and that loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: originators comply with these requirements when presenting loan options to
elements.label: text

elements.text: consumers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: HOEPA Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following loans are ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae:
elements.label: text

elements.text: • a loan that is subject to the Home Ownership and Equity Protection Act of
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1994 as described in Section 32 of Regulation Z (HOEPA); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: • a loan that is part of a larger transaction that is structured in a manner
elements.label: text

elements.text: intended to circumvent the requirements of HOEPA and Section 32 of Regulation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Z.
elements.label: text

elements.text: State Higher-Priced
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certain state-defined higher-priced loans are ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae,
elements.label: text

elements.text: regardless of whether the seller is subject to such state requirements as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: matter of law. Any state higher-priced loan described in B2-1.5-02, Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility, is ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Single Premium Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers may not require the borrower to purchase, and no proceeds of the loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be used to purchase, single premium credit insurance (e.g., life, disability,
elements.label: text

elements.text: accident, unemployment, or health insurance) or a single fee debt cancellation
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prepayment Penalties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans subject to prepayment penalties are ineligible for sale to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Arbitration
elements.label: text

elements.text: A loan that was originated on or after October 31, 2004, and is subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: mandatory arbitration is ineligible for delivery to Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text
